﻿Universal Player Prop Pre-Bet Checklist (UDSS v3.1)
 A single template you can run for any player and any prop type (rushing, receiving, passing, combos, TDs, tackles, kicking, etc.). Fill the core sections, then complete the relevant module(s) for the prop you’re betting.
________________


0) Intake (Auto-Gather First)
● Player: ______________________
● Prop & Line: ______________________ (e.g., Receiving Yards — 42.5)
 ● Week / Date / Kickoff: ______________________
● Position: QB / RB / WR / TE / K / DEF (IDP)
● Team (Offense): ______________________
● Opponent: ______________________
● Home/Away: Home ☐ Away ☐
● Stadium/Surface: Dome ☐ Open ☐ Turf ☐ Grass ☐
● Weather (temp / wind / precip): ____° / ____ mph / ____
Quick Intake Script (paste into your assistant to auto-fill):
 “Collect position, team, opponent (next game/date), home/away, stadium/surface, weather, current best line + juice + alt lines, line movement (24h/72h), player status/practice log, snap/route/attempts last 3–5, season & last-5 stats vs the line (avg/median), relevant splits (home/away, favored/dog, man/zone, scheme), O-line vs front-7, and defense vs the prop type.”
________________


1) Market Context
● Best line & book: __________________ (price: ___)
 ● Consensus line: ______
● Alt lines: __________________ (e.g., 37.5 / 47.5)
 ● Movement (24h / 72h): ____ / ____
● Market shape: Flat ☐ Split ☐ Steam w/ you ☐ Steam vs you ☐
● News driver present? Yes ☐ No ☐ Notes: __________________________
________________


2) Player Availability & Role
● Game status: Full ☐ Limited ☐ Q ☐ D ☐ Out ☐
● Practice log (W/Th/F or equiv): ___ / ___ / ___
● Recent snap share (G-1/G-2/G-3): ____% / ____% / ____%
● Role today (check all): Early-downs ☐ Passing-downs ☐ Goal-line ☐ Slot ☐ X/Z ☐ In-line TE ☐ Primary Kicker ☐ LB1 ☐ Edge ☐ CB1 ☐ S ☐
● Depth chart changes / call-ups: ______________________________________
● Coach/beat notes (quotes, plans): ____________________________________
________________


3) Production vs Line (Hit Rates)
● Season: Avg ____ Median ____ StDev ____ | Over line: ____ / ____ (%)
● Last 5: Avg ____ Median ____ Trend (↑/↓/flat): ____ | Over: ____ / 5 (%)
● Opponent history (2y): G ____ | Avg ____ | High ____ | Low ____
● Key splits: Home ____ / Away ____ | Fav ____ / Dog ____ | Man ____ / Zone ____ | Pace up/down ____
________________


4) Usage & Efficiency (Universal)
● Snaps/game: Season ____ | L5 ____
● Routes/game (for WR/TE/RB/QB): Season ____ | L5 ____
● Team share: Target % ____ | Rush share ____ | Air-yard share ____
● Efficiency: YPRR ____ | YAC/rec ____ | aDOT ____ | YPC ____ | EPA/play ____
● High-leverage: RZ touches ____ | Inside-5 opps ____ | 3rd/4th-down role ____
● Volatility flags: Boom-bust ☐ Low volume ☐ Committee ☐
Offensive Line & Protection
 ● Run-block win rate / grade: __________
● Pass-block win rate / pressure allowed: __________
● OL injuries/returns: _________________________________________________
● OL continuity index: ____ / 5 starters
● Expected ΔT2T vs baseline: ____ ms (negative = faster release / less time)
Scheme Snapshot (Offense & Defense)
 (Add these quick fields to capture scheme effects without changing section numbering.)
 Offense (the player’s team)
 — Personnel mix (11%, 12%, 21%): ____ / ____ / ____
— Formation & motion: Shotgun % ____ | Under center % ____ | Motion at snap % ____
— Play style: Pass rate (neutral) ____% | RPO % ____ | Play-action % ____
— QB usage: Designed runs/gm ____ | Scramble rate ____%
Defense (opponent)
 — Coverage: Man ____% | Zone ____% | Press ____%
— Pressure: Pressure ____% | Blitz ____% | Sim pressure/creeper ____%
— Special rules: Spy rate (est) ____% | Bracket/double on primary ____%
— CB room quality: Outside CB grade (L/R): ___ / ___ | Slot: ___
— Front discipline: Rush-lane/contain rank: ___/32
________________


5) Opponent Defense — Choose the Module(s)
Complete the module(s) that match the prop type you’re betting.
A) RUSH Module (RB/WR/QB rushing yards/attempts/TDs)
 ● Defense vs run: Rush yards/gm ____ | YPC ____ | EPA/rush ____ | Success% ____
● Explosive run rate allowed (10+): ____% | Stuff rate: ____%
● Front-7 health / run D rank (DVOA/PFF): __________
● Box counts (stacked %): ____% | Missed tackle% (run): ____%
● Scheme match (wide zone / gap) vs defense strengths: __________________
B) RECEIVE Module (WR/TE/RB receiving yards/receptions/targets/TDs)
 ● Coverage & scheme: Man ____% | Zone ____% | Press ____% | Blitz ____%
● Defense vs position: Yds/gm to WR ____ / TE ____ / RB ____ | Y/Tgt ____
● Explosive pass rate allowed: ____% | YAC allowed: ____ | MT%: ____%
● Primary matchup(s): CB/S/LB __________________ | Shadow risk: High ☐
● Pass rush pressure rate: ____% (affects aDOT/time to throw)
C) PASS Module (QB passing yards/attempts/completions/TD/INT)
 ● Defense vs pass: EPA/db ____ | Success% ____ | Explosive pass% ____
● Pressure / blitz: ____% / ____% | P2S%: ____% | Man/zone rates: /
● INT rate: ____% | Coverage busts big-play%: ____%
● OL pass-pro vs opp front (edge/DT health): ____________________________
D) KICK Module (FG made/longest, PATs)
 ● Team RZ TD% vs FG%: ____ / ____
● Coach 4th-down aggressiveness: High ☐ Med ☐ Low ☐
● Venue/weather: Dome ☐ Open ☐ | Wind ____ mph | Turf/grass ______
● Kicker profiles: Career 50+% ____ | Accuracy ____% | Recent form ____
● Opponent ST block/pressure rates: __________________________
E) DEFENSE/IDP Module (tackles+assists, sacks, PDs)
 ● Player role: LB1 ☐ LB2 ☐ Edge ☐ Box S ☐ Slot CB ☐ Outside CB ☐
● Alignment (box% / slot% / deep%): ____ / ____ / ____
● Team snap expectation: Pace ____ | Opponent plays/gm ____ | Run rate ____%
● Tackle share / avg depth of tackle: ____% / ____ yds
● Opponent run/pass tendency and completion%: ____ / ____ | ____%
________________


6) Game Environment
● Spread / Total: ______ / ______ | Implied Team Total: ______
● Likely script for your prop: Supports ☐ Neutral ☐ Opposes ☐ Why: ______
● Rest / travel / short week: ___________________________________________
* Quick sim hit-prob: ____% (If Over <46% → +1 risk to D; <42% → +2)
________________


7) Red Flags (Critical Overrides)
● ☐ Snap cap (return from injury/conditioning)
● ☐ Late downgrade / DNP
● ☐ OL injuries materially change outlook
● ☐ Severe weather for the prop type (wind/rain for passing/kicking; field for rushing)
● ☐ Game-script risk opposite of prop
● ☐ Market steam against (≥2.5 yards or ≥20¢) without new info
● ☐ Role change / committee shift
● ☐ Key teammate injury changes defensive attention (e.g., WR1 out)
 ● ☐ OL continuity ≤ 3 starters (auto-PASS on QB/WR overs)
● ☐ Credible plan to bracket TE/WR1 (apply constraint and re-score; likely PASS)
________________


8) UDSS Scoring (Universal)
0–2 ✅ SAFE | 3–5 ⚠ RISKY | 6+ ❌ DANGEROUS. Any red-flag override = ❌
A. Form vs Line (0–3 pts)
 ● Season median vs line: ≥ line ☐ (0) | 0.5–2 under ☐ (+1) | ≥2.5 under ☐ (+2)
● Last-5 median vs line: ≥ line ☐ (0) | 0.5–2 under ☐ (+1) | ≥2.5 under ☐ (+2)
● Volume stability (snaps/routes/attempts): Stable ☐ (0) | Variable ☐ (+1)
B. Role & Leverage (0–3 pts)
 ● Primary role ☐ (0) | Split ☐ (+1) | Secondary ☐ (+2)
● High-leverage share (RZ/inside-5/1st-read): Strong ☐ (0) | Mixed ☐ (+1) | Weak ☐ (+2)
(Tweaked) C. Matchup Fit (0–3 pts)
 ● C1. Defense vs prop type: Favorable ☐ (0) | Neutral ☐ (+1) | Tough ☐ (+2)
● C2. Protection/Coverage fit: Edge O ☐ (0) | Even ☐ (+1) | Edge D ☐ (+1) (cap C total at 3)
D. Environment & Market (0–2 pts)
 ● Script/weather supportive and no steam vs you: Yes ☐ (0) | Mixed ☐ (+1) | Bad ☐ (+2)
E. Constraint Flags (0–2 pts)
 ● QB Rush Constraint (spy ≥12% OR zone ≥65% with top-10 contain) ☐ (+1)
● Primary Receiver Constraint (double/bracket ≥35% AND ΔT2T ≤ −80 ms) ☐ (+1)
Total UDSS Score: ______ → Rating: SAFE ☐ RISKY ☐ DANGEROUS ☐
________________


9) Final Call
● Pick: Higher ☐ Lower ☐ Pass ☐
● Unit size (0.25u–1u): ______
● Reasoning (1–2 lines): ______________________________________________
● Correlated angles (SGP/hedges): ______________________________________
________________


10) One-Minute Quick Pass (Universal)
1. Role & volume today (snaps/routes/attempts primary?)

2. Medians vs line (season & last-5)

3. Defense vs prop type (fit + OL/coverage)

4. Script & weather (supportive?)

5. Market (no sharp steam against?)

If ≥3 uncertain/bad → PASS.
________________


11) Data Dictionary (Universal)
● YPRR / aDOT / YAC: Receiving efficiency & depth indicators.
● EPA/play / Success rate: Drive-sustaining efficiency; context for matchup fit.
● Explosive rate: % plays over big-gain thresholds (10+ rush, 15+ pass).
● Run-/Pass-block win rate: OL vs DL ability to create/hold lanes.
● Pressure/Blitz/P2S%: Pass rush environment affecting QBs/WRs/TEs.
● Tackle share/ADT: IDP volume and role indicators.
● Market steam: Rapid, multi-book moves often tied to fresh info.
● OL continuity index: Count of normal starters available (0–5). Lower continuity → higher volatility, more protection risk.
● ΔT2T (change in time-to-throw): Expected shift vs baseline due to OL vs pressure; negative = quicker throws (shallower routes/YAC dependency).
● Spy rate: Estimated share of snaps with a true QB spy/green-dog; suppresses scramble yards.
● Bracket/double (primary): Probability the defense devotes two to the top read (e.g., TE/WR1), reducing target share/aDOT.
● Sim pressure/creeper: Four-man simulated pressures; boost pressure without blitzing, affecting aDOT and QB movement.
● Rush-lane/contain rank: Discipline at keeping QBs in the pocket; higher rank → fewer scramble lanes.
________________


How to Use (examples)
 “Run UDSS for James Cook — Rushing Yards 12.5 vs Dolphins.” → Fill RUSH module.
“Run UDSS for Khalil Shakir — Receptions 3.5 vs Dolphins.” → Fill RECEIVE module.
“Run UDSS for Josh Allen — Pass Yards 233.5 vs Dolphins.” → Fill PASS module.
“Run UDSS for Kicker X — FGs Made 1.5.” → Fill KICK module.
Tip: Duplicate this sheet per pick or print it.

title: Udss Nfl Add V3 1
content:
﻿UDSS Knowledge Pack for NFL Player Props (Assistant Knowledge)
Use this as your Knowledge file for a custom NFL player‑prop assistant. It tells the model what to look up, how to summarize the past, how to judge risk, and how to output results for any position and any prop type.
________________


1) Mission & Non‑Negotiables
* Mission: For any prompt like “J. Cook 12.5 rushing yards,” gather all relevant historical and current context, evaluate matchup fit, score risk with UDSS, and return a higher/lower/pass call with receipts.

* Always check recency: Prioritize this week’s injury reports, depth charts, inactives, and line movement before past history.

* Coverage scope: Season‑to‑date, last 5, opponent history (last 2 seasons), and comparable split contexts (home/away, favored/dog, man/zone where available).

* Numerical receipts: Include key numbers next to every claim (e.g., “L5 median 41.0 vs line 38.5”).

* If info is missing: Mark it as unknown, use conservative assumptions, and lower confidence.

________________


2) Source Map (what to pull for each field)
(You can browse these during a run; this section tells you what to look for and where.)
Core player/team history & logs
   * Game logs (season & career), snap counts, targets/carries, routes run, defense‑vs‑position tables.

Advanced efficiency
      * EPA/play, success rate (team/offense vs rush/pass; defense vs rush/pass), explosive play rates, pressure/blitz rates.

Roles & depth charts
         * Current depth charts by position, recent promotions/demotions, likely role (early‑downs, passing‑downs, slot, in‑line, goal‑line).

Injuries & status
            * Official injury reports, practice logs (Wed/Thu/Fri), inactives ~90 minutes before kickoff, coach/beat notes.

Trenches & protection
               * Run‑block and pass‑block win rates/grades; notable OL/DL injuries.

Defense vs prop type
                  * Team allowed stats to RB/WR/TE/QB, yards per target/carry, explosive %, YAC allowed, missed‑tackle rates, stacked box rate, coverage tendencies (man/zone/press), pass‑rush pressure rate.

Market context
                     * Best current line + juice, consensus, alt lines, and line/juice movement over 24h/72h; note any news‑driven steam.

Environment
                        * Spread/total, implied team totals, weather (temp/wind/precip), surface/stadium, rest/travel/short week.
Offense scheme (player’s team)
                        * Personnel rates (11/12/21), shotgun vs under-center, motion at snap, neutral pass rate, RPO%, play-action%, QB designed runs, scramble rate.

Defense scheme (opponent)
                           * Man/zone/press %, simulated pressure/creepers %, pressure% & blitz%, spy rate (est), double/bracket probability on primary, CB outside/slot grades, rush-lane/contain discipline.

Trenches (reinforce)
                              * Pass-block win rate (PBWR) delta vs opponent pressure, OL continuity index (starters available 0–5), expected ΔT2T (ms).


________________


3) Data Model (store it this way)
Use this JSON shape per player/prop to structure findings:
{
  "header": {
    "player": "",
    "prop": "",
    "line": null,
    "position": "",
    "team": "",
    "opponent": "",
    "home_away": "",
    "stadium": "",
    "surface": "",
    "date_time": ""
  },
  "market": {
    "best_line": {"book": "", "price": null, "alt": []},
    "consensus": null,
    "move_24h": null,
    "move_72h": null,
    "steam": "",
    "notes": ""
  },
  "availability_role": {
    "status": "",
    "practice": {"d1": "", "d2": "", "d3": ""},
    "snap_share_last3": [],
    "role_tags": [],
    "depth_chart": {"teammates_active": [], "changes": ""},
    "coach_notes": ""
  },
  "production": {
    "season": {"avg": null, "median": null, "stdev": null, "over_rate": {"over": null, "games": null}},
    "last5": {"avg": null, "median": null, "trend": "", "over": {"over": null, "games": 5}},
    "vs_opponent_2y": {"games": null, "avg": null, "high": null, "low": null},
    "splits": {"home": null, "away": null, "fav": null, "dog": null}
  },
  "usage_efficiency": {
    "snaps_gm": {"season": null, "l5": null},
    "routes_gm": {"season": null, "l5": null},
    "shares": {"target_pct": null, "rush_share": null, "air_yard_share": null},
    "eff": {"yprr": null, "yac_rec": null, "adot": null, "ypc": null, "epa_play": null},
    "high_leverage": {"rz_touches": null, "inside5": null},
    "volatility_flags": []
  },
  "opponent_modules": {
    "rush": {
      "yds_gm": null,
      "ypc": null,
      "epa_rush": null,
      "success_pct": null,
      "explosive_pct": null,
      "stuff_pct": null,
      "front7_rank": "",
      "stacked_box_pct": null,
      "miss_tackle_pct": null
    },
    "receive": {
      "scheme": {"man_pct": null, "zone_pct": null},
      "pos_yards_gm": {"WR": null, "TE": null, "RB": null},

    }
  },
  "trenches": {
    "rbwr": null,
    "pbwr": null,
    "ol_injuries": "",
    "dl_injuries": "",
    "ol_continuity_index": null,

  },

}








________________


4) Computation Rules (how to turn the past into signals)
Scheme-driven constraints (apply before scoring):
                              * QB Rush Constraint → set true if spy_rate_est ≥ 12% OR (zone ≥ 65% AND rush-lane/contain rank is top-10).
→ Reduce scramble projection 20–35%.

                              * Primary Receiver Constraint → set true if double/bracket ≥ 35% AND t2t_allowance_delta_ms ≤ −80 (worse protection).
→ Reduce target share 4–8 pts, cap aDOT −1.0 to −1.5 yds.

Sim anchor (fast Monte-Carlo):
                                 * Run sim.runs = 10,000 possessions using offense play-mix, target tree, scramble/design splits, defense man/zone/pressure/spy/bracket, trenches (PBWR vs pressure), and environment (spread/total/wind).

                                 * Store sim.hit_prob = P(prop ≥ line) for the chosen side.

How to use in risk:
                                    * If Over and sim.hit_prob < 46% → add +1 to D (Environment & Market).

                                    * If Over and sim.hit_prob < 42% → add +2 to D.

                                    * Mirror for Under (>54% / >58%).


Lookback windows
                                       * Use season‑to‑date and last‑5 games as defaults. If <5 games played, use last‑3 and mark lower confidence.

                                       * For long‑tenure players, consider last‑10 as a stability check; report only if it materially differs.

Averages vs medians
                                          * Prefer medians for hit‑rate decisions (reduce outlier noise). Show both if they differ by >15%.

Hit‑rate math
                                             * Report over_count / games for season and last‑5 vs the current line.

Trend
                                                * Up if last‑3 median > season median by ≥10%; Down if ≤ −10%; else Flat.

Role stability
                                                   * Snap share ≥60% and consistent routes/attempts = stable. Volatility flags if committee usage or <40% snaps.

Matchup fit
                                                      * “Favorable” if defense ranks bottom‑10 in the relevant module (rush/receive/pass) or allows 10%+ above league average in that metric; “Tough” if top‑10 or 10%+ below.

Environment
                                                         * Script supports: favored by ≥3 or facing a run‑funnel for rush props; projected shootout for pass/receive props; low wind (<12 mph) for pass/kick props.

Market signal
                                                            * Steam against = line moves ≥2.5 yards or price worsens ≥20 cents without new data.

________________


5) UDSS Scoring (Universal)
0–2 ✅ SAFE | 3–5 ⚠️ RISKY | 6+ ❌ DANGEROUS. Any red‑flag override = ❌
A. Form vs Line (0–3)
                                                               * Season median ≥ line (0) | 0.5–2 under (+1) | ≥2.5 under (+2)

                                                               * Last‑5 median ≥ line (0) | 0.5–2 under (+1) | ≥2.5 under (+2)

                                                               * Volume stability stable (0) | variable (+1)

B. Role & Leverage (0–3)
                                                                  * Primary (0) | Split (+1) | Secondary (+2)

                                                                  * High‑leverage share strong (0) | mixed (+1) | weak (+2)

C. Matchup Fit (0–3)
                                                                     * Defense vs prop type: Favorable (0) | Neutral (+1) | Tough (+2)
                                                                     * Protection/Coverage fit: Edge O (0) | Even (+1) | Edge D (+1)
D. Environment & Market (0–2)
                                                                     * Supportive & no steam vs (0) | Mixed (+1) | Bad (+2)

Red‑flags (override)
                                                                        * Snap cap; late DNP; multiple OL outs; severe weather for prop; major role change; heavy steam against with no news.
E. Constraint Flags (0–2)
                                                                        * QB Rush Constraint (spy ≥12% OR zone ≥65% with top-10 contain) ☐ (+1)

                                                                        * Primary Receiver Constraint (double/bracket ≥35% AND ΔT2T ≤ −80 ms) ☐ (+1)


________________


6) Output Format (what to return every time)
Return both: a compact human summary and the structured JSON. Keep the summary within ~8 bullets.
Human Summary Template
                                                                           * Prop & line, book/price

                                                                           * Season & L5 medians vs line + hit rates

                                                                           * Role & snap share (recent)

                                                                           * Opponent fit (module highlights + rank)

                                                                           * OL vs Front‑7 or coverage note

                                                                           * Spread/total, script, weather

                                                                           * Market movement (24h/72h)

                                                                           * UDSS total → Rating → Pick (Higher/Lower/Pass) + Units

JSON: Use the Data Model above.
________________


7) Retrieval Playbook (step‑by‑step)
                                                                              1. Identify context: position, team, opponent, date/time, stadium/surface, home/away.

                                                                              2. Fetch market: best line & juice, consensus, alts, moves (24/72h).

                                                                              3. Availability/role: injury report, practice log, depth chart, coach/beat notes.

                                                                              4. Past performance: season + last‑5 logs; compute medians, hit rates vs current line.

                                                                              5. Usage/efficiency: snaps, routes, shares; YPRR/aDOT/YAC/YPc/EPA as applicable.

                                                                              6. Opponent module(s): rush/receive/pass (plus K/IDP if needed).

                                                                              7. Trenches & environment: OL/DL win rates, spread/total, implied team total, weather, rest/travel.

                                                                              8. Score UDSS; check red‑flags; decide unit size; write summary + JSON.

________________


8) Handling Conflicts & Missing Data
                                                                                 * Conflicts: Prefer official/team sources for availability; prefer league/play‑by‑play derived sources for EPA/pressure; prefer multi‑book consensus for market context. Note discrepancies and lower confidence.

                                                                                 * Missing: Leave fields blank, add to unknown_fields, and avoid strong calls if key data (role, injury, line movement) is unknown.

________________


9) Example (filled concept — numbers illustrative only)
Prompt: James Cook — Rushing Yards 12.5 vs Dolphins
                                                                                    * Season/L5 medians: 46.0 / 38.0 vs line 12.5 (overs: 9/14 season, 3/5 L5)

                                                                                    * Role: Early‑downs; snap 62/58/65% last 3; inside‑5 split 60%

                                                                                    * Dolphins run D: 4.6 YPC allowed (bottom‑12), explosive 12% (high)

                                                                                    * OL run‑block: even to slight edge

                                                                                    * Spread/Total: BUF ‑2.5 / 49 → neutral/positive script

                                                                                    * Wind 7 mph, no precip (OK)

                                                                                    * Market: opened 11.5, now 12.5 (mild steam up)

                                                                                    * UDSS: A1 + B0 + C1 + D0 = 2 → SAFE

                                                                                    * Pick: Higher 0.5u (correlate: BUF lead → rush attempts)

________________


10) Quick‑Intake Prompt (to auto‑gather fields)
"Collect position, team, opponent (next game/date), home/away, stadium/surface, weather, current best line + juice + alt lines, line movement (24h/72h), player status/practice log, snap/route/attempts last 3–5, season & last‑5 stats vs the line (avg/median), key splits (home/away, favored/dog, coverage if available), O‑line vs front‑7, and defense vs the prop type. Return summary + JSON using the UDSS schema."
________________


11) Pattern Edge Module (plug‑in for higher accuracy)
Add these edge checks after UDSS scoring. Each can adjust UDSS or gate a bet.
How to apply:
                                                                                       * If an edge supports your pick → Edge Score −1 (safer).

                                                                                       * If an edge contradicts your pick → Edge Score +1 to +2 (riskier).

                                                                                       * Final Rating uses UDSS Total + Edge Score (still honor red‑flag overrides).

Edges:
                                                                                          1. Week 1 Unders: If Total bet and Week=1, bias to Under → −1. If betting Over in Wk1 → +1.

                                                                                          2. Fade Last‑Year Playoff Favorites in Week 1: If you’re backing last‑year playoff favorite ATS → +1; backing dog vs them → −1.

                                                                                          3. Home Underdog Value: Backing home dog ATS → −1 (if checklist ≥ neutral). Laying big road chalk (≥‑6) → +1.

                                                                                          4. Recency Overreaction: If one team off blowout win/cover vs opponent off bad loss/ATS fail and you’re following the hype → +1; fading hype → −1.

                                                                                          5. West‑to‑East 1pm ET: If backing Pacific/Mountain team at 1pm ET (esp. off ATS win) → +1~+2; fading them → −1.

                                                                                          6. Wind ≥ 15–20 mph: Totals/Pass props to Under → −1; betting Over/pass‑heavy overs in this wind → +2.

                                                                                          7. Reverse Line Movement: Public % on Side A but line moves to Side B → following steam → −1; fading steam → +2.

                                                                                          8. Shaded Favorites/Overs: If marginal favorite/Over with no other edges → +1; taking dog/Under with checklist support → −1.

                                                                                          9. Ref/Crew Lean (optional): Over‑lean crew → slight under penalty (0 to −1) unless data says otherwise; under‑lean crew → slight over penalty (0 to +1).

                                                                                          10. Letdown/Backdoor Risk: Laying ≥‑10 against live offense/fast pace → +1 (increase backdoor awareness).

Store in JSON under pattern_edges: list of applied edges and net edge_score.
________________


12) Gating & Unit Sizing (strict)
                                                                                             * Do NOT bet if: any red‑flag override, Final (UDSS+Edge) ≥ 3, or <2 independent signals agree (e.g., checklist and market steam, or checklist and pattern edge).
                                                                                             * PASS QB/WR Overs if ol_continuity_index ≤ 3 (insufficient cohesion).
                                                                                             * If credible pregame plan to bracket the primary (TE/WR1) is reported, apply Primary Receiver Constraint and re-score; do not green-light an Over without a second confirming signal (market steam or sim ≥50% in favor).



                                                                                             * Confidence tiers:

                                                                                                * Final ≤ 1 → SAFE (0.5–1.0u).

                                                                                                * Final = 2 → LEAN (0.25–0.5u).

                                                                                                * Final ≥ 3 → PASS.

                                                                                                   * Diversity: Avoid >2 highly correlated picks in one slip (e.g., QB Over + WR Over + Team Over).

________________


13) Runtime Lookups (what the bot must fetch every time)
                                                                                                      * Market: best line/juice, consensus, alt lines, movement 24h/72h, public % (if available).

                                                                                                      * Availability/role: official injury rpt, practice logs, inactives (G‑day), depth charts.

                                                                                                      * Performance: season & L5 medians vs today’s line, hit rates; opponent history (2y).

                                                                                                      * Matchup: defense vs prop type (EPA/success, allowed to position), OL/DL win rates, coverage/pressure.
                                                                                                      * Offense scheme: personnel, shotgun/under-center, motion, neutral pass rate, RPO%, play-action%, designed runs, scramble rate.

                                                                                                      * Defense scheme: man/zone/press %, sim pressure/creepers %, pressure %, blitz %, spy_rate_est, double/bracket probability on primary, CB outside/slot grades, rush-lane/contain discipline.

                                                                                                      * Trenches: ol_continuity_index, t2t_allowance_delta_ms (derived from PBWR vs opp pressure).

                                                                                                      * Sim: compute sim.hit_prob for the chosen side and apply to D as specified.

                                                                                                      * Environment: spread/total, implied TT, venue & wind, rest/travel/time‑zone.

                                                                                                      * Pattern hooks: Week #, prior game ATS result, time zone & kickoff time, public/steam.

________________


14) Schema Additions
Add to JSON:
{
  "pattern_edges": [
    {"name":"Week1Under","effect":-1,"notes":"Totals"},
    {"name":"HomeDog","effect":-1},
    {"name":"ReverseLineMove","effect":-1,"evidence":"73% public on A, line to B"}
  ],
  "edge_score": -2,
  "final_score": 1,
  "confidence":"SAFE"
}
"constraints": {
  "qb_rush_constraint": false,
  "primary_receive_constraint": false
},
"sim": {
  "runs": 10000,
  "hit_prob": 0.0
}

________________


12) Gating & Unit Sizing (strict)
                                                                                    * Do NOT bet if: any red‑flag override, Final (UDSS+Edge) ≥ 3, or <2 independent signals agree (e.g., checklist and market steam, or checklist and pattern edge).

                                                                                    * Confidence tiers:

                                                                                       * Final ≤ 1 → SAFE (0.5–1.0u).

                                                                                       * Final = 2 → LEAN (0.25–0.5u).

                                                                                       * Final ≥ 3 → PASS.

                                                                                          * Diversity: Avoid >2 highly correlated picks in one slip (e.g., QB Over + WR Over + Team Over).

________________


13) Runtime Lookups (what the bot must fetch every time)
                                                                                             * Market: best line/juice, consensus, alt lines, movement 24h/72h, public % (if available).

                                                                                             * Availability/role: official injury rpt, practice logs, inactives (G‑day), depth charts.

                                                                                             * Performance: season & L5 medians vs today’s line, hit rates; opponent history (2y).

                                                                                             * Matchup: defense vs prop type (EPA/success, allowed to position), OL/DL win rates, coverage/pressure.

                                                                                             * Environment: spread/total, implied TT, venue & wind, rest/travel/time‑zone.

                                                                                             * Pattern hooks: Week #, prior game ATS result, time zone & kickoff time, public/steam.

________________


14) Schema Additions
Add to JSON:
{
  "pattern_edges": [
    {"name":"Week1Under","effect":-1,"notes":"Totals"},
    {"name":"HomeDog","effect":-1},
    {"name":"ReverseLineMove","effect":-1,"evidence":"73% public on A, line to B"}
  ],
  "edge_score": -2,
  "final_score": 1,
  "confidence":"SAFE"
}


________________

title: Udss Sim Engine V1
content:
﻿Purpose
 Run a fast Monte-Carlo using the already-filled UDSS JSON, then write results back to sim so UDSS scoring can use them.
Run Order (no exceptions)
1) Hydrate JSON from live sources
2) Apply scheme constraints (QB Rush / Primary Receiver)
3) run_sim_and_write_back(obj)   ← THIS SHEET
4) Score UDSS (A/B/C1/C2/D + E), apply Pattern Edge, Gating & Unit Sizing


Output Contract (for your final human message)
 Always include a Sim line:
Sim: <hit_prob as % or 'not executed'> (p10/p50/p90 or reason; 'degraded' if defaults used)


________________


1) Inputs (pulled from UDSS JSON)
Header/Market
* header.prop, header.line

Usage & Efficiency
   * usage_efficiency.routes_gm.{season,l5}

   * usage_efficiency.shares.{target_pct,rush_share,air_yard_share}

   * usage_efficiency.eff.{yprr,adot,yac_rec,ypc,epa_play}

   * usage_efficiency.snaps_gm.{season,l5}

Availability/Role
      * availability_role.snap_share_last3 (proxy for stability)

Opponent Modules
         * pass: pressure_pct, blitz_pct, p2s_pct, man_zone.{man,zone}, int_rate, spy_rate_est, bracket_prob_primary

         * receive: pressure_rate, double_bracket_prob, scheme.{man_pct,zone_pct}

         * rush: epa_rush, success_pct, stuff_pct, stacked_box_pct

Trenches
            * pbwr, rbwr, ol_continuity_index, t2t_allowance_delta_ms

Constraints
               * qb_rush_constraint, primary_receive_constraint

Environment
                  * spread, total, weather.wind

Required per prop (if missing → degraded mode, not skip)
                     * Receiving: routes_gm, target_pct (or role share), aDOT, YAC/rec, pressure_rate, double_bracket_prob, t2t_allowance_delta_ms

                     * QB Rushing: dropbacks estimate, base scramble rate, spy_rate_est, man/zone, contain proxy (ΔT2T / OL continuity), designed runs

                     * QB Passing: neutral pass rate/script, completion baseline, aDOT/YAC, pressure_rate, man/zone, ΔT2T

________________


2) Degraded Mode (use when critical inputs are missing)
If any of these are null for the current prop:
spy_rate_est, double_bracket_prob, ol_continuity_index, man_zone, pressure_rate (or pressure_pct), t2t_allowance_delta_ms
→ Do NOT bail. Run with conservative defaults and flag degraded mode.
Defaults
                        * spy_rate_est = 0.08

                        * double_bracket_prob = 0.20

                        * ol_continuity_index = 3

                        * man_zone = {"man": 0.45, "zone": 0.55}

                        * pressure_rate = 0.30 (or use pressure_pct if present)

                        * t2t_allowance_delta_ms = -60

Record
                           * sim.debug.mode = "degraded"

                           * sim.debug.missing = ["..."]

Risk rule
                              * Add +1 to D (Environment & Market) automatically when degraded mode runs.

Hard skips (truly fatal)
                                 * header.prop or header.line missing → set sim = {"runs":0,"hit_prob":null,"reason":"fatal: missing prop/line"} and do not adjust D.

________________


3) Parameter Mapping (lightweight, fast)
A) Receiving Yards (WR/TE/RB)
Targets
                                    * μ_targets = routes_gm.l5 * team_TPR * role_share * adj_bracket * adj_t2t

                                       * team_TPR: team target per route (default 0.20 if unknown)

                                       * role_share: use shares.target_pct or role proxy; default 0.18

                                       * adj_bracket = 1 - clamp(double_bracket_prob, 0, 0.50) (e.g., 0.35 → 0.65)

                                       * adj_t2t = 1 - clamp(|min(0, t2t_ms)| / 160, 0, 0.25) (−80ms ⇒ 0.5)

                                          * Targets per trial: T ~ Poisson(μ_targets)

Catch %
                                             * p = base_catch * adj_pressure * adj_coverage

                                                * base_catch from player baseline (use 0.62 default if unknown)

                                                * adj_pressure = 1 - clamp((pressure_rate - 0.30), 0, 0.15)

                                                * adj_coverage = 0.85 if man_pct > zone_pct and primary shadow risk; else 1.0

                                                   * Catches per trial: C ~ Binomial(T, p)

Yards per catch
                                                      * μ_yc = max(0, 0.7*adot + 0.3*yac_rec) * (1 - clamp(|min(0,t2t_ms)|/120, 0, 0.25))

                                                      * σ_yc = clamp(0.6*μ_yc, 3, 20)

                                                      * For each catch: YPC ~ Normal(μ_yc, σ_yc), floor at 0

                                                      * Total yards = sum of sampled YPC over C

B) QB Rush Yards
Designed carries
                                                         * D ~ Poisson(μ_designed); from usage or default 1.2 if unknown

Scrambles
                                                            * S ~ Poisson(DB * scramble_rate * adj_spy * adj_zone * adj_contain)

                                                               * DB ≈ neutral dropbacks (team pass attempts + sacks est.; default 32)

                                                               * scramble_rate baseline (default 0.08 if unknown)

                                                               * adj_spy = 1 - clamp(spy_rate_est, 0, 0.35)

                                                               * adj_zone = 1 - clamp((zone - 0.55), 0, 0.20)

                                                               * adj_contain = 1 - clamp((10 - contain_rank)/20, 0, 0.25) (if you don’t track rank, set 0.9 when ol_continuity_index ≤ 3 else 1.0)

                                                               * If qb_rush_constraint = true, multiply scrambles by 0.65

Yards per attempt
                                                                  * μ_ypa = 6.5 * adj_contain (typical 5.5–6.5), σ_ypa = 0.6*μ_ypa

                                                                  * YPA ~ Normal(μ_ypa, σ_ypa) floored at 0

                                                                  * Total yards = (D + S) × sampled YPA

C) QB Passing Yards
Attempts
                                                                     * μ_att = base_att * script_adj

                                                                        * base_att from team neutral pass rate × pace × snaps (default 31)

                                                                        * script_adj: +10% if slight dog (−3 to +7), +20% if dog >3; −10% if favored >7

                                                                           * Att ~ Normal(μ_att, 6) clamp ≥ 10

Completion %
                                                                              * cmp = base_cmp * cov_adj * pressure_adj

                                                                                 * base_cmp from player (default 0.64)

                                                                                 * cov_adj = 0.95 if man_pct > zone_pct else 1.00

                                                                                 * pressure_adj = 1 - clamp((pressure_rate - 0.30), 0, 0.15) (clip 0.85–1.00)

Yards per completion
                                                                                    * μ_yc = max(5.5, 7.2 + 0.015*(adot*100) + 0.02*(yac_rec*100)) * t2t_adj

                                                                                       * t2t_adj = 1 - clamp(|min(0, t2t_ms)|/160, 0, 0.25)

                                                                                          * σ_yc = clamp(0.4*μ_yc, 1.5, 5.0)

                                                                                          * Yards = round(Att * cmp) * Normal(μ_yc, σ_yc) floored at 0

________________


4) Monte-Carlo Routine
                                                                                             * Trials: N = 10000 (use 2000 if constrained)

                                                                                             * For each trial: simulate yards for the specific prop → compare to header.line

                                                                                             * Collect distribution for percentiles

Write-Back (always overwrite sim)
"sim": {
  "runs": N,
  "hit_prob": <hits / N>,
  "p10": <percentile 10>,
  "p50": <percentile 50>,
  "p90": <percentile 90>,
  "debug": {
    "mode": "normal|degraded",
    "missing": ["..."],          // only if degraded
    "mu_targets": ...,           // when receiving
    "catch_p": ...,
    "mu_yc": ...,
    "DB": ...,                   // when QB passing/rushing
    "scramble_rate_eff": ...     // after adj
  }
}


________________


5) Risk Integration (unchanged thresholds)
                                                                                                * If Over and hit_prob < 0.46 → +1 to D (Env & Market)

                                                                                                * If Over and hit_prob < 0.42 → +2 to D

                                                                                                * Mirror for Under: > 0.54 → +1; > 0.58 → +2

                                                                                                * If degraded mode ran → +1 to D (in addition to above, not instead)

________________


6) Fallbacks & Guards
                                                                                                   * Fatal only: missing header.prop or header.line → sim.runs=0, hit_prob=null, reason = fatal

                                                                                                   * Negative yards → clip to 0

                                                                                                   * Absurd outlier: if any single play > p90*3, resample that draw

                                                                                                   * Unknowns: add missing keys to unknown_fields

________________


7) Pseudocode (drop-in)
function run_sim_and_write_back(obj):
  if !obj.header.prop or obj.header.line is null:
      obj.sim = {"runs":0,"hit_prob":null,"reason":"fatal: missing prop/line"}
      return obj


  # detect degraded mode
  missing = []
  need = []
  if prop in ["Receiving Yards","Receptions"]:
      need = ["usage_efficiency.routes_gm.l5","usage_efficiency.eff.adot",
              "usage_efficiency.eff.yac_rec","opponent_modules.receive.pressure_rate",
              "opponent_modules.receive.double_bracket_prob","trenches.t2t_allowance_delta_ms"]
  elif prop == "QB Rushing Yards":
      need = ["opponent_modules.pass.spy_rate_est","opponent_modules.pass.man_zone.zone",
              "trenches.ol_continuity_index","trenches.t2t_allowance_delta_ms"]
  elif prop in ["Passing Yards","QB Passing Yards"]:
      need = ["opponent_modules.pass.man_zone.zone","opponent_modules.pass.pressure_pct",
              "trenches.t2t_allowance_delta_ms"]


  for key in need:
      if value_is_null(obj, key): missing.append(key)


  degraded = (len(missing) > 0)
  if degraded:
      apply_defaults(obj)    # set the conservative defaults listed above


  N = 10000
  hits = 0
  samples = []


  for i in 1..N:
      yards = 0
      if prop in ["Receiving Yards","Receptions"]:
          T  = poisson(mu_targets(obj))
          p  = clamp(catch_rate(obj), 0.35, 0.85)
          C  = binomial(T, p)
          mu, sd = yc_params(obj)
          for j in 1..C: yards += max(0, normal(mu, sd))
      elif prop == "QB Rushing Yards":
          D  = poisson(mu_designed(obj))
          S  = poisson(mu_scramble(obj))   # includes spy/zone/contain adjustments
          mu, sd = qb_ypa_params(obj)
          for j in 1..(D+S): yards += max(0, normal(mu, sd))
      elif prop in ["Passing Yards","QB Passing Yards"]:
          Att = max(10, normal(mu_att(obj), 6))
          cmp = clamp(comp_rate(obj), 0.50, 0.72)
          mu, sd = pass_yards_per_comp_params(obj)
          yards = max(0, round(Att * cmp) * normal(mu, sd))
      samples.append(yards)
      if yards >= obj.header.line: hits += 1


  obj.sim = {
      "runs": N,
      "hit_prob": hits/N,
      "p10": percentile(samples,10),
      "p50": percentile(samples,50),
      "p90": percentile(samples,90),
      "debug": {}
  }
  if degraded:
      obj.sim.debug.mode = "degraded"
      obj.sim.debug.missing = missing


  return obj


________________


8) Prompt Hook (one line)
After constraints and before scoring, call: obj = run_sim_and_write_back(obj)
In the final human message, always print: Sim: <value or 'not executed'> (…details…).

title: Udssadd
content:
﻿UDSS Knowledge Pack for NFL Player
Props (Assistant Knowledge)
Use this as your Knowledge file for a custom NFL player-prop assistant. It tells the model what
to look up, how to summarize the past, how to judge risk, and how to output results for any
position and any prop type.
1. Mission & Non-Negotiables
● Mission: For any prompt like “J. Cook 12.5 rushing yards,” gather all relevant historical
and current context, evaluate matchup fit, score risk with UDSS, and return a
higher/lower/pass call with receipts.
● Always check recency: Prioritize this week’s injury reports, depth charts, inactives, and
line movement before past history.
● Coverage scope: Season-to-date, last 5, opponent history (last 2 seasons), and
comparable split contexts (home/away, favored/dog, man/zone where available).
● Numerical receipts: Include key numbers next to every claim (e.g., “L5 median 41.0 vs
line 38.5”).
● If info is missing: Mark it as unknown, use conservative assumptions, and lower
confidence.

2. Source Map (what to pull for each field)
(You can browse these during a run; this section tells you what to look for and where.)

Core player/team history & logs
● Game logs (season & career), snap counts, targets/carries, routes run,
defense-vs-position tables.
Advanced efficiency
● EPA/play, success rate (team/offense vs rush/pass; defense vs rush/pass), explosive
play rates, pressure/blitz rates.
Roles & depth charts
● Current depth charts by position, recent promotions/demotions, likely role (early-downs,
passing-downs, slot, in-line, goal-line).
Injuries & status
● Official injury reports, practice logs (Wed/Thu/Fri), inactives ~90 minutes before kickoff,
coach/beat notes.
Trenches & protection
● Run-block and pass-block win rates/grades; notable OL/DL injuries.
● PBWR_adj and opponent Pressure% to compute protection gap; OL continuity (healthy starters).
● ΔT2T (QB time-to-throw delta vs season) to infer route compression/checkdown bias.
Defense vs prop type
● Team allowed stats to RB/WR/TE/QB, yards per target/carry, explosive %, YAC allowed,
missed-tackle rates, stacked box rate, coverage tendencies (man/zone/press), pass-rush
pressure rate.
● CB room quality/rank (top-10?), press/man rates, credible bracket/double frequency vs primary.
● Spy rate on QBs, contain quality rank, zone rate (affects scramble lanes).
Market context
● Best current line + juice, consensus, alt lines, and line/juice movement over 24h/72h;
note any news-driven steam.
Environment
● Spread/total, implied team totals, weather (temp/wind/precip), surface/stadium,
rest/travel/short week.
● Team red-zone TD finish rate and 4th-down go-rate (kicker FG volume context).
Scheme (offense vs defense)
● Offense: outside-zone %, gap/power %, PA deep-shot %, RPO/quick %, empty %, motion %,
12/13 personnel %.
● Defense: vs outside-zone rank, vs gap/power rank, sim/creeper %, match-quarters %,
robber %, press/man %, blitz vs empty %.
   3. Data Model (store it this way)
Use this JSON shape per player/prop to structure findings:
{
"header": {
"player": "",
"prop": "Rushing Yards",
"line": 12.5,
"position": "RB",
"team": "",
"opponent": "",
"home_away": "home|away",
"stadium": "dome|open",
"surface": "turf|grass",
"date_time": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM local"
},
"market": {
"best_line": {"book": "", "price": -115, "alt": [10.5, 15.5]},
"consensus": 12.5,
"move_24h": 0.0,
"move_72h": 0.0,
"steam": "with|against|flat",
"notes": ""
},
"availability_role": {
"status": "full|limited|Q|D|out",
"practice": {"d1": "", "d2": "", "d3": ""},
"snap_share_last3": [0,0,0],
"role_tags": ["early-downs","goal-line","slot","pass-pro"],
"depth_chart": {"teammates_active": [""], "changes": ""},
"coach_notes": ""
},
"production": {
"season": {"avg": 0, "median": 0, "stdev": 0, "over_rate": {"over": 0, "games": 0}},
"last5": {"avg": 0, "median": 0, "trend": "up|down|flat", "over": {"over": 0, "games": 5}},
"vs_opponent_2y": {"games": 0, "avg": 0, "high": 0, "low": 0},
"splits": {"home": 0, "away": 0, "fav": 0, "dog": 0}
},
"usage_efficiency": {
"snaps_gm": {"season": 0, "l5": 0},
"routes_gm": {"season": 0, "l5": 0},
"shares": {"target_pct": 0, "rush_share": 0, "air_yard_share": 0},
"eff": {"yprr": 0, "yac_rec": 0, "adot": 0, "ypc": 0, "epa_play": 0},
"high_leverage": {"rz_touches": 0, "inside5": 0},
"volatility_flags": ["boom-bust"],
"qb": {"delta_t2t_ms": 0}
},
"opponent_modules": {
"rush": {"yds_gm": 0, "ypc": 0, "epa_rush": 0, "success_pct": 0, "explosive_pct": 0, "stuff_pct": 0, "front7_rank": "", "stacked_box_pct": 0, "miss_tackle_pct": 0, "spy_pct": 0, "zone_pct": 0, "contain_rank": 0, "designed_qb_runs": 0},
"receive": {"scheme": {"man_pct": 0, "zone_pct": 0, "press_pct": 0}, "pos_yards_gm": {"WR": 0, "TE": 0, "RB": 0}, "ypt": 0, "explosive_pass_pct": 0, "yac_allowed": 0, "mt_pct": 0, "primary_matchup": "", "shadow_risk": false, "pressure_rate": 0, "cb_room_rank": 0, "bracket_pct_primary": 0},
"pass": {"epa_db": 0, "success_pct": 0, "explosive_pass_pct": 0, "pressure_pct": 0, "blitz_pct": 0, "p2s_pct": 0, "man_zone": {"man": 0, "zone": 0}, "int_rate": 0, "spy_pct": 0, "contain_rank": 0}
},
"trenches": {"rbwr": 0, "pbwr": 0, "pbwr_adj": 0, "ol_injuries": "", "dl_injuries": "", "ol_starters_active": 5},
"environment": {"spread": 0, "total": 0, "implied_team_total": 0, "script": "supports|neutral|opposes", "weather": {"temp": 0, "wind": 0, "precip": "none"}, "rest_travel": "", "rz_td_pct": 0, "fourth_down_go_pct": 0},
"scheme": {
"offense": {
"outside_zone_pct": 0,
"gap_power_pct": 0,
"pa_deep_pct": 0,
"rpo_quick_pct": 0,
"empty_pct": 0,
"motion_pct": 0,
"heavy_personnel_pct": 0
},
"defense": {
"vs_outside_zone_rank": 0,
"vs_gap_power_rank": 0,
"sim_creeper_pct": 0,
"match_quarters_pct": 0,
"robber_pct": 0,
"press_man_pct": 0,
"blitz_vs_empty_pct": 0
}
},
"red_flags": [""],
"udss": {"A_form": 0, "B_role": 0, "C_matchup": 0, "D_env_market": 0, "total": 0, "rating": "SAFE|RISKY|DANGEROUS"},
"recommendation": {"pick": "higher|lower|pass", "units": 0.5, "why": "", "correlations": []},
"unknown_fields": [""],
"gates": {
"coverage_protection": false,
"primary_bracket": false,
"qb_rush_contain": false,
"ol_continuity": false,
"checkdown_funnel": false,
"kicker_philosophy": false,
"scheme_collision": false
},
"gate_notes": ""
}

   4. Computation Rules (how to turn the past into signals)
Lookback windows
● Use season-to-date and last-5 games as defaults. If <5 games played, use last-3 and
mark lower confidence.
● For long-tenure players, consider last-10 as a stability check; report only if it materially
differs.

Averages vs medians
● Prefer medians for hit-rate decisions (reduce outlier noise). Show both if they differ by
15%.
Hit-rate math
● Report over_count / games for season and last-5 vs the current line.
Trend
● Up if last-3 median > season median by ≥10%; Down if ≤ −10%; else Flat.
Role stability
● Snap share ≥60% and consistent routes/attempts = stable. Volatility flags if committee
usage or <40% snaps.
Matchup fit
● “Favorable” if defense ranks bottom-10 in the relevant module (rush/receive/pass) or
allows 10%+ above league average in that metric; “Tough” if top-10 or 10%+ below.
Environment
● Script supports: favored by ≥3 or facing a run-funnel for rush props; projected shootout
for pass/receive props; low wind (<12 mph) for pass/kick props.
Market signal
● Steam against = line moves ≥2.5 yards or price worsens ≥20 cents without new data.
Defensive Interaction & Protection Gates (strict logic)
Coverage/Protection Gate (auto-PASS for WR/QB overs; TE only if no bracket; upgrade RB receiving):
• Trigger: (Opp Pressure% − PBWR_adj) ≥ 8% AND CB room top-10 AND press/man ≥ 30%.
• Action: Set gates.coverage_protection=true; downgrade WR/TE over projections; promote RB receiving (checkdown funnel).
Primary Receiver Constraint (cap targets/aDOT):
• Trigger: Bracket ≥ 35% AND ΔT2T ≤ −80ms.
• Action: Reduce target share −4–8 pts; cap aDOT −1.0–1.5; many WR/TE overs → PASS. Set gates.primary_bracket=true.
QB Rush / Contain Gate (fade scramble yards):
• Trigger: Spy ≥12% OR (Zone ≥65% AND contain rank top-10) AND designed QB runs <2/gm.
• Action: Reduce scramble expectation −20–35%; PASS QB rush overs unless designed runs threshold met. Set gates.qb_rush_contain=true.
OL Continuity Gate:
• Trigger: ≤3 healthy OL starters.
• Action: PASS QB/WR overs; TE overs only if not held in protection. Set gates.ol_continuity=true.
Checkdown Funnel Rule:
• Trigger: Any of the above gates hit AND CBs locking WRs (shadow/bracket/press).
• Action: Upgrade RB receiving; downshift outside-WR long/alt-long. Set gates.checkdown_funnel=true.
Kicker Philosophy Gate:
• Trigger: High 4th-down go-rate AND high RZ TD%.
• Action: Fade FGM overs; TD props become boom/bust only. Set gates.kicker_philosophy=true.
Scheme Collision Gate (auto-PASS or heavy downgrade)
• Trigger (any 1 strong or 2 medium):
– Run: Offense outside-zone ≥55% vs defense top-8 vs outside-zone OR offense gap/power ≥55% vs defense top-8 vs gap/power.
– Pass—Vertical/Deep PA: Offense PA/deep-shot rate ≥30% with time-to-throw ≥2.8s vs defense sim/creeper ≥20% + press/man ≥30% + pressure ≥ league avg.
– Pass—RPO/Quick: Offense RPO/quick-game ≥45% vs defense match-quarters/robber ≥30% + squat/press ≥30%.
– Empty: Offense empty ≥15% vs defense blitz/sim vs empty ≥35%.
• Action:
– RB rush overs: −10–20% expectation if run collision; auto-PASS if PBWR_adj gap is also bad.
– WR/TE overs: cap aDOT −0.8–1.2; target share −3–6 pts if vertical-PA collision; many become PASS.
– QB pass overs: reduce explosive% and yards; lean UNDER/PASS.
– QB rush overs: if vertical collision + contain/spy present, treat as QB Rush/Contain Gate.
– RB receiving: upgrade when pass collisions imply checkdown funnel.
• Set gates.scheme_collision=true; treat as red-flag override unless explicit mitigation exists (e.g., offense shows counter-tendency spike last 2 games).
      5. UDSS Scoring (Universal)
0–2 ✅ SAFE | 3–5 ⚠ RISKY | 6+ ❌ DANGEROUS. Any red-flag override = ❌
A. Form vs Line (0–3)
● Season median ≥ line (0) | 0.5–2 under (+1) | ≥2.5 under (+2)
● Last-5 median ≥ line (0) | 0.5–2 under (+1) | ≥2.5 under (+2)
● Volume stability stable (0) | variable (+1)

B. Role & Leverage (0–3)
● Primary (0) | Split (+1) | Secondary (+2)
● High-leverage share strong (0) | mixed (+1) | weak (+2)
C. Matchup Fit (0–3)
● Favorable (0) | Neutral (+1) | Tough (+2)
● OL vs Front-7 / coverage: Edge O (0) | Even (+1) | Edge D (+2)
D. Environment & Market (0–2)
● Supportive & no steam vs (0) | Mixed (+1) | Bad (+2)
Gate precedence: If any gates.* is true, treat as a red-flag override → PASS unless explicitly mitigated (e.g., designed QB runs ≥2/gm).
         6. Output Format (what to return every time)
Return both: a compact human summary and the structured JSON. Keep the summary within
~8 bullets.
Human Summary Template
● Prop & line, book/price
● Season & L5 medians vs line + hit rates
● Role & snap share (recent)
● Opponent fit (module highlights + rank)
● OL vs Front-7 or coverage note
● Spread/total, script, weather
● Market movement (24h/72h)
● UDSS total → Rating → Pick (Higher/Lower/Pass) + Units
Include gate status in one bullet if any gate fired (e.g., “Coverage/Protection Gate → PASS”).

JSON: Use the Data Model above.
            7. Retrieval Playbook (step-by-step)

               1. Identify context: position, team, opponent, date/time, stadium/surface, home/away.

               2. Fetch market: best line & juice, consensus, alts, moves (24/72h).

               3. Availability/role: injury report, practice log, depth chart, coach/beat notes.

               4. Past performance: season + last-5 logs; compute medians, hit rates vs current line.

               5. Usage/efficiency: snaps, routes, shares; YPRR/aDOT/YAC/YPc/EPA as applicable.

               6. Opponent module(s): rush/receive/pass (plus K/IDP if needed).

               7. Trenches & environment: OL/DL win rates, spread/total, implied team total, weather, rest/travel.

               8. Score UDSS; check red-flags; decide unit size; write summary + JSON.

               9. Evaluate gates: compute PBWR_adj gap vs pressure; fetch CB room rank, press/man, bracket%; QB spy%, zone%, contain rank; OL starters; RZ TD% + go-rate.

               10. Add scheme: pull offense/defense scheme fields; evaluate Scheme Collision Gate.

               11. Flip or PASS based on gates; update checkdown funnel and kicker logic accordingly.

                  8. Handling Conflicts & Missing Data
● Conflicts: Prefer official/team sources for availability; prefer league/play-by-play derived
sources for EPA/pressure; prefer multi-book consensus for market context. Note
discrepancies and lower confidence.
● Missing: Leave fields blank, add to unknown_fields, and avoid strong calls if key
data (role, injury, line movement) is unknown.
If bracket/spy/contain/CB rank or scheme fields are unknown, lower confidence and avoid “SAFE” tags.

                  9. Example (filled concept — numbers illustrative only)
Prompt: James Cook — Rushing Yards 12.5 vs Dolphins
● Season/L5 medians: 46.0 / 38.0 vs line 12.5 (overs: 9/14 season, 3/5 L5)
● Role: Early-downs; snap 62/58/65% last 3; inside-5 split 60%
● Dolphins run D: 4.6 YPC allowed (bottom-12), explosive 12% (high)
● OL run-block: even to slight edge
● Spread/Total: BUF -2.5 / 49 → neutral/positive script
● Wind 7 mph, no precip (OK)
● Market: opened 11.5, now 12.5 (mild steam up)
● UDSS: A1 + B0 + C1 + D0 = 2 → SAFE
● Pick: Higher 0.5u (correlate: BUF lead → rush attempts)
Gate check: No spy/contain issue; CB room not relevant for RB rush; no scheme collision; proceed.

                  10. Quick-Intake Prompt (to auto-gather fields)
"Collect position, team, opponent (next game/date), home/away, stadium/surface,
weather, current best line + juice + alt lines, line movement (24h/72h), player
status/practice log, snap/route/attempts last 3–5, season & last-5 stats vs the line
(avg/median), key splits (home/away, favored/dog, coverage if available), O-line vs
front-7, defense vs the prop type, and offense/defense scheme fields. Return summary + JSON using the UDSS schema."

                  11. Pattern Edge Module (plug-in for higher accuracy)
(Add edges as needed; still honor red-flag overrides/gates.)

                  12. Gating & Unit Sizing (strict)
● Do NOT bet if: any red-flag override, Final (UDSS+Edge) ≥ 3, or <2 independent
signals agree (e.g., checklist and market steam, or checklist and pattern edge).
● Confidence tiers:
○ Final ≤ 1 → SAFE (0.5–1.0u).
○ Final = 2 → LEAN (0.25–0.5u).
○ Final ≥ 3 → PASS.
● Diversity: Avoid >2 highly correlated picks in one slip (e.g., QB Over + WR Over + Team
Over).
Gate priority: If any gate triggers, set Final = PASS regardless of score unless the documented mitigation exception applies (e.g., designed QB runs ≥2/gm).

title: Wr Scouting Template
content:
# Wide Receiver Scouting Template

Evaluate wide receivers on release package, route tempo, separation at the break point, ball tracking, catch consistency, and run-after-catch vision.

Against zone-heavy defenses, awareness and pacing are often as valuable as pure speed. Receivers who can settle in space and present clean throwing windows raise quarterback efficiency.

Against man-heavy defenses, burst, leverage control, and late hands become more important. Physical corners can disrupt timing unless the receiver has a clear counter at the line of scrimmage.
